revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明石国造]]
{{Infobox Japanese clan | clan_name = Akashi Kokuzō | crest_caption = | ancestral_name = [[Ama clan]] | founder = [[Tomi no Misokuni]] | classification = [[Shinbetsu]] ([[Chigi]], earthly deity) | origin = [[Yamato Province]] | stronghold = [[Harima Province]], [[Akashi District]], Akashi Village | notable_members = See [[#Notable Figures]] and [[#Descendants]] | branches = Ama clan, Nishi-Ama clan }} {{ill|Akashi Kokuzo|ja|明石国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akashi Kokuzo|1=Akashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108487216}} (あかしのくにのみやつこ・あかしこくぞう) were the {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|qid=Q2483673|lt=Kokuzo|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}}, or provincial governors, who governed {{ill|Akashi Province|ja|明石国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akashi Province|1=Akashi Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512480}}, an ancient province located in what is now the eastern part of {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|qid=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}. This area includes present-day {{ill|Eastern Harima|ja|東播磨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eastern Harima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shinmei|ja|神明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei|1=Shinmei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590276}}, and {{ill|Miki, Hyōgo|en|Miki, Hyōgo|qid=Q31238|lt=Miki City|1=Miki, Hyōgo}} in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. == Overview == === Ancestor === According to the "{{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q133833194}}" in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, '''Tomi no Jisoku no Miyatsuko''' (都弥自足尼), a descendant of the same lineage as the {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|en|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qid=Q11387078|lt=Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, was appointed as the Kokuzo of Akashi Province. === Clan === The Akashi Kokuzo belonged to the '''{{ill|Ama Clan|ja|海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (あまうじ), bearing the {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|qid=Q1664099|lt=kabane|1=Kabane}} of "Na" (直). In the third year of {{ill|Jingo-keiun|en|Jingo-keiun|qid=Q1373049|lt=Jingokeiun|1=Jingo-keiun}}, they were granted the surname '''Yamato Akashi no Muraji''' (大和赤石連). It is theorized that the later {{ill|Akashi Clan|ja|明石氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akashi Clan|1=Akashi Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108865128}}, a branch of the {{ill|Akamatsu clan|en|Akamatsu clan|qid=Q2659667|lt=Akamatsu Clan|1=Akamatsu clan}}, who claimed descent from the {{ill|Murakami Genji|ja|村上源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Genji|1=Murakami-Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3867344}}, were actually descendants of the Akashi Kokuzo.<ref>[[Ryō Ōta]], "Akashi," in *{{ill|Seishi Kakei Daijiten|ja|姓氏家系大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishi Kakei Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* (1942), p. 31.</ref> Related clans include the {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|en|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qid=Q11387078|lt=Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, {{ill|Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|久比岐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kubiki Kokuzo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97383587}}, and the {{ill|Aomi Clan|ja|青海氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Territory === The Akashi Kokuzo ruled from the '''Akashi District''' (明石郡) in Harima Province. To their north were the {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108487215}}, and to the west, across the Amakawa River, were the {{ill|Harima no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. === Shrine === The clan's tutelary deity was enshrined at the {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|en|Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|qid=Q11559098|lt=Wadatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|1=Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)}}. This {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, designated as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, is the only such shrine in Akashi District and honors {{ill|Wadatsumi|ja|海神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadatsumi|1=water deity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1916821}}, the ancestral deity of the clan. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kaifu|en|Kaifu|qid=Q446149|lt=Ama Clan|1=Kaifu}} {{Japan-hist-stub|qq=}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} {{Japan-hist-stub|qq=}} {{pref-stub-Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}} ==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi|qq=}} ==Footnotes== ===Notes=== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ===References===
{{translated page|ja|明石国造|version=98717525|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|
